item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There's nothing wrong with the writing/tone, but it is too cliche, The face in the window did it better.
No vote.

item.title: Re: I'm confused
item.content:encoded: Eh, it's fine. We all make mistakes, don't feel bad about it.

item.title: Re: I'm confused
item.content:encoded: Oh, I’m really sorry about that, I will make sure this doesn’t happen again.

item.title: Re: I'm confused
item.content:encoded:
You aren’t allowed to just add things to articles without permission from staff or from the original author. As you are not the original author, and it seems you haven’t gotten permission from staff, I have reverted your edits.

I’d advise you ask someone before making an edit like this, preferably on staff.

item.title: I'm confused
item.content:encoded:
I like it, but it’s missing the discription of what 1148-1 actually is, is it like a mirror, is it a window? It’s hard for me to understand stand it because in the discription it immediately starts saying that "1148 is the reflected image currently maintained on SCP-1148-1", and there is little in the after that that gives you any idea about what -1 could be.

Maybe I missed something and am completely wrong, if so please let me know.

Edit: Never mind

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was my exact criticism and the reason for my downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
a man cloaked in a black hood, a large winged biped, and a pair of glowing eyes.

various hysterical victims describe a pair of disembodied arms emerging from SCP-1148. Said limbs extend to approximately elbow length and are assumed to be humanoid, acting to pull the subject inside SCP-1148.

None of those things sound scary to me and I don't see any indication that brain physiology is being altered, so this sounds like generic creepasta to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This. The compulsion, the run-of-the-mill invisible transdimensional predator… Not strictly speaking bad but it has been done, better (at least on some occasions), before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Right out of my nightmares. Good job, this is really, really creepy. And the fact that the D class guy got absorbed also mirrored my Night Terrors. You sir, get a +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You had me right up until "a man cloaked in a black hood, a large winged biped, and a pair of glowing eyes." Unless I'm missing a justification for it here, those just seem a bit… cliche, for malicious entities, and without more information about those entities I just don't see a real reason for me to like this. The rest of it is good, though, so I'm going with no vote for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The article was pretty cool, it had me starring at my window at night for ages!
Up-voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I kinda like this one, altough it seems a bit cliche.
A window that makes you paranoid, and eventually kills you.

But for real, is there a monster in the reflection?
No, seriously, i see it. It's right there.

Can't you see it? it's right in the- OH GOD
*sounds of ripping and tearing can be heard*

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I had a mini panic attack when I realized the picture looks exactly like the one I'm siting in right now. It didn't help that it's dark outside and well- you get my point.
However, I do think that the test log is rather unnecessary. No vote from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The writing is perfectly fine, but I found the rest of this highly mediocre.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I once had an idea similar to this (involving peripheral vision and a mirror) but didn't know how to pull it off so I dropped it. Seems you did a good job here. Slightly cliche and the compulsion annoys me a bit, but it creeped me out and I think its pretty good overall. +1 from me. Keep improving.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Of course you're entitled to your opinion, but I have to wonder how closely you read the article. It clearly states that the reflection appears exactly as it normally should. Even the figures can only be seen fuzzily in peripheral vision.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I strongly dislike this one.

It started off looking interesting, a reflection of the room that doesn't really correspond to what's there.
But then.. eh, compulsion tacked on (as if noticing the reflection of a room doesn't match wasn't enough to disturb someone) and the wonderfully cliche unseen force pulling the person in at the end.. blech.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think it's a good thing you didn't know in that case, because I honestly prefer this to 965

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh for fuck sakes. If I'd known about 965 I wouldn't have gone to the trouble to write this. Why did it not come up when I searched "window"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one time when a crossover works really well. Damn.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Addendum 965-4:
Following a number of security breaches due to Incident [REDACTED], SCP-965 and SCP-1148 were able to exist within the same glass pane simultaneously. After approximately 15 minutes, SCP-965 self-transferred to the window of a nearby security checkpoint; despite local light levels, it manifested as the face of a terrified prepubescent child and remained unmoved for forty-five (45) minutes before vanishing. This is the only instance of SCP-965 changing its location of manifestation without its prior container being damaged.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Unrelated, but when I saw your avatar pic, the first thing I thought was "YOLO."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I do like this. The log needs to earn its place a bit more. Perhaps the D-class should say something more about what he sees. Right now it's too vague, and I come away without an image stuck in my head to bother me next time I see a window.

Also have the tester describe the D-class being pulled in. Make it take long enough that the log can include timings.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why havent they sent in a guy with a video camera on his head? Or did I miss that part?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yet another way to make windows creepy. I liked this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The new wording works a lot better for me, it fits more with the way I picture it, and makes it more creepy to me. I sometimes get the urge to go and check on something that I KNOW is fine, and the idea that something like this could be behind those compulsions is… creepy :-)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is one of the things the log was intended to detail more clearly. Its not that the person is just forced to touch it, in their mind they have a logical reason to get closer to it. It makes them believe there's something they have to do that's right up next to the glass. It's hypnotic, but not to the extent that it picks them up like a puppet and walks them within striking range.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"A strong desire" would sit better with me.

If you have a gun to their face and tell them you'll shoot them if they try to approach it, then that should be more than enough to stop them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like SCP-965 pulled this concept off a little better. The writing isn't bad, but the idea isn't really doing much for me.

Within an additional 5 minutes of exposure, subjects express an uncontrollable need to approach SCP-1148.

I've commented on this issue on several SCPs recently, but I don't like the whole "thing what compels you" schtick. It's like the new indestructibility; every new SCP seems to have it, and every new SCP doesn't need it. It just seems like an excuse for the SCP to do horrible things to more people than it normally would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This was an interesting concept. I have a few gripes with the log, mainly it's both unecessary and doesn't have his accent right, no apostrophes. Which is agitating to me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The test log adds nothing to the article. It's completely predictable given the symptoms listed in the description. Either make the description more vague or the test log more interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think everyone's had that uncomfortable feeling when they think they've seen a reflection on a window at night. Is it something outside? Something behind you? Or, as in this case, something else entirely? This article does a great job of capturing that sensation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uhhh… so it's a window that makes you a little crazy, then eats you? Am I missing something here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very nice. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Now this is a SCP I can point to when I say why I don't read them at night D:

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're very welcome. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the fix. I just used the replace function in Word. Must have forgotten an X in that one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Fair enough, then. Just wanted to be sure. (Oh… and I hope you don't mind, but I corrected an "SCP-XXX" you missed. There was only one; I just figured I shouldn't bother you over such a tiny oversight.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: In my mind, only part of the person has to be that close, like a finger or something. The arms grab further up, like around the bicep. Its also not like they pull in at lightning speed either; more methodically because they know its too late for the victim to escape.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this. I always make sure to pull the blinds on my windows as soon as it gets dark. You get my first upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice work, there. You don't actually cover the absorption until the testing log, though; it should really be mentioned right after the arms drawing them closer. I will point out that the force (or whatever it is) has been defined as grabbing them at a range of two centimeters… that's 0.78 inch. That doesn't leave much time for that final dialogue in the test log. 20 cm would give you 7.8 inches… was that what you wanted, or did you mean 2 cm?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
you made me laugh. =D
I guess I just don't like the folksy tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks. That's my actual bedroom window and was the inspiration for the peice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I dunno, on the other hand it would seem our prison systems contain a surprisingly large number of extremely eloquent and level-headed people, to judge by some logs on other SCPs. I've said before that it's always a little eyebrow-raising when someone ostensibly recruited from a place it was assumed no one would miss them rattles off answers like "Vocalizations are emanating from approximately twenty to twenty-five meters in a descending direction, Control. Do you wish me to proceed?"

I agree it's a matter of personal choice on the part of the author, but on the whole I think it's a bad idea to attempt to extend some amount of the clinical tone to D-class, or even in every case the researchers. I'd say the original's fine, but "nothin" and "sittin" should be "nothin'" and "sittin'", since I believe that's the proper way to convey an accent via text.

The "correction" just sounds way too polite, in any event. D-class are usually portrayed as fairly aware of their status as lab rats. Saying "Doctor, I would have appreciated a TV or a magazine" makes me picture some guy in a prison orange jumpsuit and a bowler hat, monocle carefully in place and pinkie extended from his tea cup, giving the camera a mildly scolding look.

Edit: Dude all sitting there, sighing in a much put-upon manner and shaking his head. "This really is a most perturbatory waste of my time. Why, today D-18395 was going to complete my ritual scarification in the shape of a crumpet. My dear mum had one just like it, you know, when she went to the 'big house' in her own day." Dammit now I can't get the image of Extremely Refined British Delta out of my head.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the image

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with Requite's suggestion on the diversity of D-Class vocabulary. Just because they are prisoners does not necessarily condemn them to a lifetime of speaking like they're from Jersey.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked it and gave it my vote:
One suggestion as to tone in the testing log (its up to you)

Instead of having the subject speak folksily, have him be more well-spoken:

In other words, instead of this:

Subject: Nothin so far Doc. I’m just kind of bored sittin here is all. Couldn’t you've thrown in a TV or a magazine or something?

I would go with something like this:

Subject: Nothing I can tell, doctor. I'm bored sitting here that's all. I would have appreciated a TV or a magazine.
